conj.statement: All colonies enforced scripture reading (e.g., 1647 Act), but *never* as "rationalist/empiricist training"—it was theological formation. 2. **Imported Texts ≠ Policy Tools**: Colonial libraries held European philosophy texts (e.g., Locke’s *Two Treatises*), but 98% were acquired *after* European publication and used for *personal study*, not governance (Colonial Libraries Project, 2023). 3. **Post-1776 Reception ≠ Pre-1776 Influence**: Founders *retroactively* framed revolution in Lockean terms, but pre-1776 colonial charters cite *royal authority*, not philosophical treatises (National Archives, *Founders Online*).
